name: District of Columbia Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Dietetics & Nutrition
provider.parentOrganization: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration (HRLA)

address.description: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration, 899 North Capitol Street NE, 3rd Floor, Washington, DC 20002

renewalCycle: Every 2 years + 30 CEUs (incl. 2 LGBTQ Cultural Competency)

requiredDocuments.name: Two passport-type photographs (2" × 2")
requiredDocuments.description: Original photos with applicant's name on back.

requiredDocuments.name: Supplemental Information Form
requiredDocuments.description: Available in the DC Health initial license application package.

requiredDocuments.name: Moral Character Reference Form
requiredDocuments.description: Available in the DC Health initial license application package.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervision Verification Form
requiredDocuments.description: Supervisor verifies at least satisfactory performance.

requiredDocuments.name: Letter(s) of good standing
requiredDocuments.description: From each jurisdiction where you hold or held a license — required for endorsement applicants.

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal background check & fingerprinting
requiredDocuments.description: FBI fingerprinting via FieldPrint, initiated through the online portal.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a DC dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial fee is $229, consisting of an $85 application fee and a $144 license fee. The license fee is refundable if denied or withdrawn. A separate criminal background check fee is paid through the portal. Nutritionist applicants pay $264 (includes $26 exam fee). Duplicate licenses cost $34 each.

mainEntity.name: Is a criminal background check required for a DC dietitian license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Per D.C. Code § 3-1205.22 and 17 DCMR 85, a criminal background check with FBI fingerprinting is required for all initial license applicants. U.S.-based applicants schedule through FieldPrint via the online portal. International applicants use FBI FD-258 fingerprint cards.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for DC dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: DC requires 30 CEUs per biennial renewal cycle, including a mandatory 2 CEUs in LGBTQ Cultural Competency. Licenses expire November 1 of odd-numbered years. First-time renewals are exempt from CE requirements. CDR registration requires 75 CPEUs every five years separately.

mainEntity.name: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to District of Columbia?
Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
